content:
How professional JavaScript developers build scalable interfaces instead of chasing DOM methods.

4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Part 1 was about understanding the DOM as a tree, this part is about something even more important.
Scale!!
Almost every DOM tutorial teaches you how to update one button.
Professional applications rarely update one button.
They update hundreds of elements, thousands of rows, multiple forms, dashboards, notifications, charts, sidebars, and modals — all at the same time.
The difference isn’t writing more code.
It’s writing smarter code.
These are the ideas that completely changed how I build JavaScript applications.
Stop Attaching Event Listeners Everywhere
One of my earliest projects had around 200 dynamically generated cards.
Every card had a delete button.
My solution?
document.querySelectorAll(".delete-btn").forEach(button => {
button.addEventListener("click", removeCard);
});It worked.
